Do not use single-letter variable names except in trivial loop counters (`i`, `j`).
Do not declare multiple variables on the same line (e.g., `int a, b, c;`).
Do not use `var` when the type is ambiguous and readability suffers.
Do not use public fields; use properties instead.
Do not shadow outer variables with inner declarations of the same name.
Do not leave unused variables in the code; remove or prefix with a discard `_`.
Do not use magic numbers or magic strings; extract them into named constants or configuration.
